paper_title: Taxonomic and Taphonomic Analysis of Small Mammal Assemblages at Hohle Fels

methods.description: Process for identifying and categorizing small mammal remains from archaeological sites to understand past environments and biodiversity.

steps.description: Sediments are excavated from 25 cm² sub-quadrants and water-screened on site using water pumped from the nearby Ach River.
steps.searchable_summary: excavate and water-screen sediments from sub-quadrants

steps.description: The remaining material is sorted by material type and size, and bones are removed and sorted by specimen size or broad taxonomic category.
steps.searchable_summary: sort remaining material by type and size, categorize bones
outputs: sorted bones by size and category

steps.description: Examine all specimens at 10x -50x magnification using a Euromax binocular microscope.
Identify taxonomic categories using isolated dental elements and select post-cranial elements via direct comparison with osteological collections and published data.
steps.searchable_summary: identify taxa using dental and post-cranial elements

steps.description: Calculate diversity indices including richness (NTAXA) and heterogeneity (reciprocal of Simpson's Index, 1/D).
Produce rarefaction curves using NTAXA data for each geological horizon in PAST 3.14.

methods.description: Detailed analysis of faunal remains to identify accumulation modes and taxonomic biases, and to interpret past environments and human behaviors.

steps.description: Document bone and tooth modifications including fragmentation, surface cracking, edge rounding, root etching, abrasion, weathering, thermal discolouration, oxide staining, and digestive corrosion.
Calculate relative abundances for each skeletal element using the formula R = N i /(MNI x E i ).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate relative abundances of skeletal elements

steps.description: Apply Kendall's Tau to compare relative element abundances with actualistic data.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Kendall's Tau for abundance comparison

steps.description: Document digestive corrosion on incisors, molars, and select post-cranial elements.
steps.searchable_summary: document digestive corrosion on dental and post-cranial elements

steps.description: Calculate the proportion of individuals allocated to each vegetative/climatic niche and compare these percentages within and between horizons.
Employ the habitat weighing method to distribute each small mammal species into habitat types based on modern distributions.
steps.searchable_summary: apply habitat weighing method for species distribution
